name: BTC Asymmetric Trend Persistence v1
thesis: BTC 4h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for testing trend continuation with asymmetric exit logic where trigger, risk, and exit indicators play different roles. In a chop-dominant environment, the goal is to capture only brief persistence rather than assume full trend follow-through.
edge_mechanism: broad persistence continuation with asymmetric exits
invalidation_condition: Abort if broad persistence entries are too sparse, if value holds fail to extend after entry, or if the asymmetric exit structure cannot improve post-cost outcomes.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price remains aligned with fast value after a directional push, indicating short-lived persistence rather than immediate reversal. Entry uses the hold, but risk and exit are managed by different components.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Separating trigger, risk, and exit roles avoids one-indicator-everything logic. Even in chop, some holds persist long enough to monetize if exits are asymmetric and realistic.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is a cleaner test bed than TAO for this role-separated continuation because persistence structure is more readable and less gap-like.

indicator_role_map.EMA_20: entry trigger via post-push value hold

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market remains fully rotational, value holds may not extend enough to justify continuation entries even with asymmetric exits.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trade but the back half underperforms, keep the trigger and replace the runner with a harder second target.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; hold and participation qualifier are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed through broad post-push hold logic instead of exact breakout choreography

long: Exit remaining 25% on close below EMA_55 or 5 bars
short: Exit remaining 25% on close above EMA_55 or 5 bars
